textSample: Work Services Pricing About Start a pilot → Pipeline from content. Built for B2B SaaS founders and their teams. Founder LinkedIn ghostwriting, content marketing, and SEO. No paid ads. No retainer. Start my 30-day pilot → No retainer commitment. No long contracts. $1,500 to $2,000 for 30 days. See what the output looks like → B2B SAAS COMPANIES WE'VE WORKED WITH Archive Radar launch: 2.45% engagement rate · 7 brands to waitlist B2B SaaS influencer campaign: 855,000 impressions · 3.40% engagement The real problem isn't your product. You're spending on content. You're spending on influencers. But when you pull the metrics, the investment doesn't justify the return. If there's even any return. Meanwhile your competitor, the one with the worse product and half your budget, is getting all the engagement. Their content is everywhere. Their name comes up in every conversation. That's not a product problem. That's a marketing problem. Budget spent. No pipeline. You've paid for posts, campaigns, and creator fees. The CAC math doesn't work. Better product. Less visibility. Your roadmap is stronger. Your pricing is tighter. But no one outside your current users knows you exist. Their name. Not yours. Every time your ICP searches, reads, or asks around, your competitor's name is the one that comes up. This is a solvable problem. Most agencies just aren't solving it. INDUSTRY RECOGNITION Webby Awards Best B2B Campaign Cannes Lions Shortlist Recognition Three ways we work with you. We build the founder's personal brand and the company's content machine. At Seed to Series A, you need both. All of them start the same way: a 30-day pilot so you can see the work before you commit. Foundation $1,800/month A founder LinkedIn presence and content foundation, built for the stage where you don't have a marketing team yet. 12 ghostwritten LinkedIn posts/month. 2 SEO articles/month. Content calendar. Monthly performance report. For: Seed-stage B2B SaaS founders with no marketing hire. Learn more → Most popular Engine $3,500/month A content system that builds organic pipeline. Founder brand plus company SEO, working together. Founder LinkedIn ghostwriting (3x/week + 2 carousels/month). Brand page management. 4 to 6 SEO articles/month. Monthly KPI report. Quarterly strategy session. For: Series A B2B SaaS founders and their teams that need leads from content, not just presence. Learn more → Full Stack $6,000/month The complete content machine. Founder brand, company SEO, distribution, and attribution so you can prove what's working. Everything in Engine plus technical SEO, distribution layer (email + carousel repurposing), attribution setup, and one original research asset per quarter. For: Series A/B founders and their teams ready to treat content as a growth engine, not a line item. Learn more → Need a content audit, a technical SEO review, or pSEO for your product? We do those too. See pricing → No paid ads. No video. No junior teams. We don't run paid ads. We don't produce video. We don't have a floor of junior coordinators managing your account while the senior team stays busy winning new clients. What we do: founder LinkedIn ghostwriting, content strategy, SEO. We do it at a level most agencies can't match precisely because we're not spread thin. We're a handful of people who do things really well. You talk directly to whoever is doing the work. Every time. How it works. STEP 1 The Pilot We start with 30 days. You see the quality of the work. We learn your product, your ICP, and your voice. $1,500 to $2,000, no strings attached. STEP 2 The Strategy If we continue, we build the full program: content calendar, creator roster, or both. Strategy first, execution second. No guessing. STEP 3 The Output You get the results. We handle the work. You own everything we produce: content, relationships, playbooks. Results, not promises. Most of our client relationships start with the pilot. Most of them don't stop there. All from one client. One year. Four diff

title: Archive Radar Launch: Output Marketing
metaDescription: Human-first B2B marketing for SaaS and DTC brands. Influencer programs, content strategy, LinkedIn, and SEO. Start with a 30-day pilot.
h2: Building a product launch? Start with a pilot.
See what the output looks like.
buttons: Services
textSample: Work Services Pricing About Start a pilot → ARCHIVE Radar Feature Launch Product Launch Marketing · February 2025 The company Archive is a B2B SaaS platform for DTC brands. Creator discovery, analytics, and influencer campaign tracking, all in one place. The challenge In early 2025, Archive launched Radar: a new feature for brands to get early visibility on emerging creators before they break out. The challenge was making that launch land with the right people, not just make noise. The problem A new feature inside an existing product is one of the hardest things to market. Existing customers already have a mental model of what the product does. New prospects don't know it exists. The window for early-adoption energy is short. Archive needed real waitlist demand, not just impressions. And it needed to prime the top-tier DTC brands on that list for an upsell conversation. The execution • Value-first positioning: Radar was framed as early access with VIP advantage, not just another feature announcement. • Tiered customer messaging: different content for existing customers (Slack community), prospects (LinkedIn), and investors. • Community activation: announced in Archive's existing Slack community to warm the list before going broader. • Lead generation mechanic: $250 early-access investment as a qualifying signal. • Multi-channel coordination: LinkedIn content, email, Slack, and direct outreach running in sequence. The numbers 7,363 impressions 4,757 members reached 2.45% engagement rate 7 brands added to waitlist 68 comments 12 reposts 1 upsell opportunity identified What worked and why The 2.45% engagement rate on a product launch post is strong. The industry average for B2B LinkedIn content sits around 0.5 to 1%. That gap came from specificity. The Slack community activation drove the first wave of waitlist signups. Identifying Midori Bikinis as a potential upsell came from tracking engagement quality, not just volume. If your next product launch matters, this is the playbook. Building a product launch? Start with a pilot. Start my 30-day pilot → No retainer. See all case studies → See what the output looks like. Start your 30-day pilot → Prefer direct email: vanessa@output-marketing.com Work · Services · Pricing · About · Contact LinkedIn © 2026 Output Marketing · Privacy Edit with

title: Archive AI Newsletter: Output Marketing
metaDescription: Human-first B2B marketing for SaaS and DTC brands. Influencer programs, content strategy, LinkedIn, and SEO. Start with a 30-day pilot.
h2: Building a retention program? Start with a pilot.
See what the output looks like.
buttons: Services
textSample: Work Services Pricing About Start a pilot → ARCHIVE AI Insider Newsletter Product Launch & Customer Retention · 2025 The challenge B2B SaaS retention runs on perceived value between purchase and renewal. Customers who only hear from you when something breaks or when renewal is due don't renew. Archive needed a content program that gave customers a reason to stay engaged, one that spoke differently to each tier. What we did • Built a 3-tier segmentation model: premium tier (VIP positioning), standard tier (value emphasis), lower tier (exclusive content to drive upsell) • Multi-channel coordination: email per tier, LinkedIn posts, Slack announcements, creator partnerships, and blog content • Established recurring newsletter cadence with AI-specific content relevant to the ICP • Created a platform for ongoing thought leadership The outcomes • 1 new recurring revenue stream established • 3 customer tiers segmented and activated • Multi-channel coordination across email, LinkedIn, Slack, creators, and blog • Retention improved through value-driven content between product touchpoints What worked and why The segmentation model was the most important decision. A single newsletter to all tiers would have underperformed for everyone. No single channel reaches the whole customer base reliably. Email has the highest intent. LinkedIn has the highest visibility. Slack has the highest trust. Running them in coordination is what made the program work. If your customers go quiet between purchases, this is where to start. Building a retention program? Start with a pilot. Start my 30-day pilot → See all case studies → See what the output looks like. Start your 30-day pilot → Prefer direct email: vanessa@output-marketing.com Work · Services · Pricing · About · Contact LinkedIn © 2026 Output Marketing · Privacy Edit with

title: Archive LinkedIn Strategy: Output Marketing
metaDescription: Human-first B2B marketing for SaaS and DTC brands. Influencer programs, content strategy, LinkedIn, and SEO. Start with a 30-day pilot.
h2: Need LinkedIn content that actually gets engagement?
See what the output looks like.
buttons: Services
textSample: Work Services Pricing About Start a pilot → ARCHIVE LinkedIn Content Strategy Thought Leadership & Content Marketing · Ongoing Context Archive sells to DTC brands. Its ICP: brand managers, CMOs, and founders at DTC companies. They spend meaningful time on LinkedIn. A credible LinkedIn presence for Archive wasn't nice to have. It was the organic channel with the highest concentration of their buyers. The challenge Thought leadership content is easy to produce and easy to ignore. Archive needed content that generated engagement from the right people: real positions, backed with data, in a voice that sounded like someone who knew the space. What we did • Built content pillars: creator economy insights, AI in influencer marketing, micro-influencer strategy, contrarian takes on DTC marketing • Wrote from Archive's brand voice: data-backed, direct, and opinionated • Kept a consistent publishing cadence for algorithm momentum • Tracked performance at the post level: reactions, comments, shares, and engagement rate The numbers (featured post) 7,363 impressions 114 reactions 68 comments 12 reposts 1.21% engagement rate maintained What worked and why The posts that drove the most engagement weren't trend reports or product features. They were contrarian takes. "What's actually dying in influencer marketing" outperforms "5 influencer marketing trends to watch" every time. 1.21% on B2B LinkedIn content is above benchmark. The industry average sits around 0.5%. The difference comes from posting less often but making each post worth reading. If your ICP is on LinkedIn and you're not showing up, this is what consistent looks like. Need LinkedIn content that actually gets engagement? Start my 30-day pilot → See all case studies → See what the output looks like. title: Archive Product Marketing: Output Marketing

buttons: Services
textSample: Work Services Pricing About Start a pilot → ARCHIVE Product Marketing Campaign Product Marketing · Ongoing Context Archive builds tools for DTC brands to manage influencer marketing. Their product line expanded to include an AI Assistant and a Gifting campaign feature. Both needed product marketing: not just an announcement, but a full-funnel program that moved DTC brand prospects from awareness to trial. The challenge Product marketing for a SaaS platform is different from campaign marketing. The goal isn't a spike in impressions. It's a system that generates pipeline. Running two product campaigns simultaneously while keeping the messaging distinct for each required coordination that most agencies can't execute cleanly. What we did • Built a full campaign dashboard giving Archive visibility into both campaigns in one place • Ran AI Assistant campaign: positioned Archive's AI as a practical tool, anchored to the DTC buyer's workflow • Gifting campaign: designed for brands already using product gifting who hadn't built a repeatable system • Tracked pipeline end-to-end from content impression to sales conversation The results • 2 simultaneous product campaigns managed • 1 full campaign dashboard built from scratch • End-to-end DTC brand pipeline tracking implemented • 0 dropped balls: both campaigns ran on schedule, on brief, and within budget What worked and why The most valuable output wasn't the content. It was the dashboard. Archive's team went from running campaigns by feel to having one place where everything tracked. Running two product campaigns simultaneously with distinct messaging required strict content separation at the brief level. The AI Assistant audience and the Gifting audience overlap but aren't the same people. Running multiple campaigns and losing track? Need a system that tracks? Start with a pilot. title: Founder LinkedIn Ghostwriting — Output Marketing
metaDescription: Human-first B2B marketing for SaaS and DTC brands. Influencer programs, content strategy, LinkedIn, and SEO. Start with a 30-day pilot.
h1: Your buyers are on LinkedIn. You're not showing up.
h2: 90% of SaaS companies post only from their company page.
See what the output looks like.
buttons: Services
Will it actually sound like me?
Do I have to write anything?
What if I want to write some posts myself?
How long until LinkedIn starts working?
Do you post on my behalf or do I post myself?
textSample: Work Services Pricing About Start a pilot → Your buyers are on LinkedIn. You're not showing up. We write in your voice. Three posts a week. Carousels that get read. LinkedIn that builds pipeline, not just followers. Start my 30-day pilot → No retainer. No long contract. $1,500 to $2,000 for 30 days. 90% of SaaS companies post only from their company page. LinkedIn's own data says personal profiles get 8 times more engagement than company pages. Yet most B2B SaaS companies treat their founder's LinkedIn as an afterthought, a place to share company announcements and the occasional thought that felt worth posting. Meanwhile, the founders who show up consistently, take real positions, and write in a voice that sounds like a person rather than a press release are generating inbound. Not because they got lucky. Because LinkedIn rewards personal content from people, not brands. The gap between what most companies do and what works is where we operate. Is this for you? Founder, Seed to Series B • Your LinkedIn is inconsistent. Or empty. Or you post when something big happens and go quiet for three weeks. • Your ICP is on LinkedIn. You know it. You see your competitors showing up there. • You have opinions about your space. You just don't have time to write. • You've thought about ghostwriting but weren't sure if the voice would sound like you. Sound familiar? That's who we built this for. What the engagement looks like. Voice capture: Two to three conversations. We listen to how you talk about your work, your product, and your industry. We read your existing writing. We find your voice before we write a word. Content calendar: A full month of planned posts, mapped to your ICP and what they care about. You approve direction before we write anything. Ghostwriting: Three posts a week. Two carousels a month. Written in your voice. You review. Nothing goes live without your approval. Performance tracking: Monthly report covering reach, engagement rate, profile views, and inbound attributed to LinkedIn activity. Iteration: We track what lands and adjust. A strong voice improves over time. Month three performs better than month one. What it produces. 8x Engagement: personal profile vs company page LinkedIn 2025 B2B Benchmark Report 1/3 Of Pylon's total pipeline came from founder LinkedIn in 2024 277% More B2B leads from LinkedIn than any other social platform WE'VE DONE THIS BEFORE. Before Output Marketing, Vanessa ran influencer programs at Fireflies.ai. She managed a B2B SaaS campaign for their Series A product push: 39 creators, 855,958 impressions, 3.40% engagement. The four Archive campaigns that followed were built on the same playbook. FROM THE WORK Archive LinkedIn Content Strategy 7,363 impressions · 114 reactions · 1.21% engagement rate Read case study → Archive Radar Feature Launch 7 brands to waitlist · 2.45% engagement rate Read case study → How it works. STEP 1 Pilot (30 days) We have the voice capture conversations, build the first month's content calendar, write the first batch of posts. You see the quality before committing to anything. STEP 2 Monthly cadence Content calendar approved. Posts written. You review. We publish. Monthly report delivered. STEP 3 Compound The first month is the hardest. By month three, the voice is locked. By month six, the inbound is real. Foundation tier $1,800 / month Or start with the pilot: $1,500 to $2,000 for 30 days. Start my 30-day pilot → See full pricing → Questions you probably have. Will it actually sound like me? Do I have to write anything? What if I want to write some posts myself? How long until LinkedIn starts working? Do you post on my behalf or do I post myself? textSample: Work Services Pricing About Start a pilot → Traffic is not the goal. Pipeline is. We target the keywords your buyers use when they're ready to buy, not when they're just looking around. B2B SaaS SEO built for lead quality. Start my 30-day pilot → No retainer. No long contract. $1,500 to $2,000 for 30 days. Most B2B SaaS SEO is optimized for rankings, not revenue. The agency publishes 8 articles a month. Traffic goes up. Demo requests don't. The client renews anyway because the traffic chart looks good. The problem is the keyword strategy. Top-of-funnel informational content ranks easily and converts almost nothing. Bottom-of-funnel content, the posts buyers find when they're actively evaluating options, converts 10 to 25 times more. Most agencies avoid it because it's harder to rank and harder to measure. That's what we focus on. Is this for you? B2B SaaS, Series A to B • You have content but it's not generating pipeline • You're ranking for terms your ICP isn't actually searching • You want to rank in traditional search AND get cited in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews • You need to show your board that content is generating demos, not just impressions Sound like you? That's who we built this for. What the work covers. Pain-Point SEO: We target high-intent keywords, the terms buyers search when they're comparing tools, looking for alternatives, or trying to solve a specific problem. Not broad informational queries. Technical audit: We find and fix what's holding your existing content back. Crawl issues, page speed, structured data, internal linking. Content strategy: Topic clusters built around buying-intent stages. Every piece has a purpose in the pipeline. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization): We structure content so it gets cited by AI tools. ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews are now part of the B2B buyer research process. Attribution: Monthly report tracking rankings, organic traffic, and leads attributed to organic search. You see which content is driving demos. What organic SEO produces for B2B SaaS. 702% Average SEO ROI for B2B SaaS companies First Page Sage, 2025 7 months Average break-even point for B2B SaaS SEO investment $290 Long-term organic CAC as content compounds vs $802 for paid search FROM THE WORK Archive LinkedIn Content Strategy 7,363 impressions · 1.21% engagement rate maintained Read case study → Archive Radar Feature Launch 2.45% engagement rate · 7 brands to waitlist Read case study → How it works. title: Archive Radar Launch: Output Marketing
metaDescription: Human-first B2B marketing for SaaS and DTC brands. Influencer programs, content strategy, LinkedIn, and SEO. Start with a 30-day pilot.
h2: Building a product launch? Start with a pilot.
See what the output looks like.
buttons: Services
textSample: ARCHIVE Radar Feature Launch Product Launch Marketing · February 2025 The company Archive is a B2B SaaS platform for DTC brands. Creator discovery, analytics, and influencer campaign tracking, all in one place. The challenge In early 2025, Archive launched Radar: a new feature for brands to get early visibility on emerging creators before they break out. The challenge was making that launch land with the right people, not just make noise. The problem A new feature inside an existing product is one of the hardest things to market. Existing customers already have a mental model of what the product does. New prospects don't know it exists. The window for early-adoption energy is short. Archive needed real waitlist demand, not just impressions. And it needed to prime the top-tier DTC brands on that list for an upsell conversation. The execution • Value-first positioning: Radar was framed as early access with VIP advantage, not just another feature announcement. • Tiered customer messaging: different content for existing customers (Slack community), prospects (LinkedIn), and investors. • Community activation: announced in Archive's existing Slack community to warm the list before going broader. • Lead generation mechanic: $250 early-access investment as a qualifying signal. • Multi-channel coordination: LinkedIn content, email, Slack, and direct outreach running in sequence. The numbers 7,363 impressions 4,757 members reached 2.45% engagement rate 7 brands added to waitlist 68 comments 12 reposts 1 upsell opportunity identified What worked and why The 2.45% engagement rate on a product launch post is strong. The industry average for B2B LinkedIn content sits around 0.5 to 1%. That gap came from specificity. The Slack community activation drove the first wave of waitlist signups. Identifying Midori Bikinis as a potential upsell came from tracking engagement quality, not just volume. If your next product launch matters, this is the playbook. Building a product launch? Start with a pilot. Start my 30-day pilot → No retainer. See all case studies → See what the output looks like. Start your 30-day pilot → Prefer direct email: vanessa@output-marketing.com Work · Services · Pricing · About · Contact LinkedIn © 2026 Output Marketing · Privacy Edit with

title: Archive AI Newsletter: Output Marketing
metaDescription: Human-first B2B marketing for SaaS and DTC brands. Influencer programs, content strategy, LinkedIn, and SEO. Start with a 30-day pilot.
h2: Building a retention program? Start with a pilot.
See what the output looks like.
buttons: Services
textSample: ARCHIVE AI Insider Newsletter Product Launch & Customer Retention · 2025 The challenge B2B SaaS retention runs on perceived value between purchase and renewal. Customers who only hear from you when something breaks or when renewal is due don't renew. Archive needed a content program that gave customers a reason to stay engaged, one that spoke differently to each tier. What we did • Built a 3-tier segmentation model: premium tier (VIP positioning), standard tier (value emphasis), lower tier (exclusive content to drive upsell) • Multi-channel coordination: email per tier, LinkedIn posts, Slack announcements, creator partnerships, and blog content • Established recurring newsletter cadence with AI-specific content relevant to the ICP • Created a platform for ongoing thought leadership The outcomes • 1 new recurring revenue stream established • 3 customer tiers segmented and activated • Multi-channel coordination across email, LinkedIn, Slack, creators, and blog • Retention improved through value-driven content between product touchpoints What worked and why The segmentation model was the most important decision. A single newsletter to all tiers would have underperformed for everyone. No single channel reaches the whole customer base reliably. Email has the highest intent. LinkedIn has the highest visibility. Slack has the highest trust. Running them in coordination is what made the program work. If your customers go quiet between purchases, this is where to start. Building a retention program? Start with a pilot. Start my 30-day pilot → See all case studies → See what the output looks like. Start your 30-day pilot → Prefer direct email: vanessa@output-marketing.com Work · Services · Pricing · About · Contact LinkedIn © 2026 Output Marketing · Privacy Edit with

textSample: ARCHIVE LinkedIn Content Strategy Thought Leadership & Content Marketing · Ongoing Context Archive sells to DTC brands. Its ICP: brand managers, CMOs, and founders at DTC companies. They spend meaningful time on LinkedIn. A credible LinkedIn presence for Archive wasn't nice to have. It was the organic channel with the highest concentration of their buyers. The challenge Thought leadership content is easy to produce and easy to ignore. Archive needed content that generated engagement from the right people: real positions, backed with data, in a voice that sounded like someone who knew the space. What we did • Built content pillars: creator economy insights, AI in influencer marketing, micro-influencer strategy, contrarian takes on DTC marketing • Wrote from Archive's brand voice: data-backed, direct, and opinionated • Kept a consistent publishing cadence for algorithm momentum • Tracked performance at the post level: reactions, comments, shares, and engagement rate The numbers (featured post) 7,363 impressions 114 reactions 68 comments 12 reposts 1.21% engagement rate maintained What worked and why The posts that drove the most engagement weren't trend reports or product features. They were contrarian takes. "What's actually dying in influencer marketing" outperforms "5 influencer marketing trends to watch" every time. 1.21% on B2B LinkedIn content is above benchmark. The industry average sits around 0.5%. The difference comes from posting less often but making each post worth reading. If your ICP is on LinkedIn and you're not showing up, this is what consistent looks like. Need LinkedIn content that actually gets engagement? It was the dashboard. Archive's team went from running campaigns by feel to having one place where everything tracked. Running two product campaigns simultaneously with distinct messaging required strict content separation at the brief level. The AI Assistant audience and the Gifting audience overlap but aren't the same people. Running multiple campaigns and losing track? Need a system that tracks? Start with a pilot. textSample: Your buyers are on LinkedIn. You're not showing up. We write in your voice. Three posts a week. Carousels that get read. LinkedIn that builds pipeline, not just followers. Start my 30-day pilot → No retainer. No long contract. $1,500 to $2,000 for 30 days. 90% of SaaS companies post only from their company page. LinkedIn's own data says personal profiles get 8 times more engagement than company pages. Yet most B2B SaaS companies treat their founder's LinkedIn as an afterthought, a place to share company announcements and the occasional thought that felt worth posting. Meanwhile, the founders who show up consistently, take real positions, and write in a voice that sounds like a person rather than a press release are generating inbound. Not because they got lucky. Because LinkedIn rewards personal content from people, not brands. The gap between what most companies do and what works is where we operate. Is this for you? Founder, Seed to Series B • Your LinkedIn is inconsistent. Or empty. Or you post when something big happens and go quiet for three weeks. • Your ICP is on LinkedIn. You know it. You see your competitors showing up there. • You have opinions about your space. You just don't have time to write. • You've thought about ghostwriting but weren't sure if the voice would sound like you. Sound familiar? That's who we built this for. What the engagement looks like. Voice capture: Two to three conversations. We listen to how you talk about your work, your product, and your industry. We read your existing writing. We find your voice before we write a word. Content calendar: A full month of planned posts, mapped to your ICP and what they care about. You approve direction before we write anything. Ghostwriting: Three posts a week. Two carousels a month. Written in your voice. You review. Nothing goes live without your approval. Performance tracking: Monthly report covering reach, engagement rate, profile views, and inbound attributed to LinkedIn activity. Iteration: We track what lands and adjust. A strong voice improves over time. Month three performs better than month one. What it produces. 8x Engagement: personal profile vs company page LinkedIn 2025 B2B Benchmark Report 1/3 Of Pylon's total pipeline came from founder LinkedIn in 2024 277% More B2B leads from LinkedIn than any other social platform WE'VE DONE THIS BEFORE. Before Output Marketing, Vanessa ran influencer programs at Fireflies.ai. She managed a B2B SaaS campaign for their Series A product push: 39 creators, 855,958 impressions, 3.40% engagement. The four Archive campaigns that followed were built on the same playbook. FROM THE WORK Archive LinkedIn Content Strategy 7,363 impressions · 114 reactions · 1.21% engagement rate Read case study → Archive Radar Feature Launch 7 brands to waitlist · 2.45% engagement rate Read case study → How it works. textSample: Traffic is not the goal. Pipeline is. We target the keywords your buyers use when they're ready to buy, not when they're just looking around. B2B SaaS SEO built for lead quality. Start my 30-day pilot → No retainer. No long contract. $1,500 to $2,000 for 30 days. Most B2B SaaS SEO is optimized for rankings, not revenue. The agency publishes 8 articles a month. Traffic goes up. Demo requests don't. The client renews anyway because the traffic chart looks good. The problem is the keyword strategy. Top-of-funnel informational content ranks easily and converts almost nothing. Bottom-of-funnel content, the posts buyers find when they're actively evaluating options, converts 10 to 25 times more. Most agencies avoid it because it's harder to rank and harder to measure. That's what we focus on. Is this for you? B2B SaaS, Series A to B • You have content but it's not generating pipeline • You're ranking for terms your ICP isn't actually searching • You want to rank in traditional search AND get cited in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews • You need to show your board that content is generating demos, not just impressions Sound like you? That's who we built this for. What the work covers. Pain-Point SEO: We target high-intent keywords, the terms buyers search when they're comparing tools, looking for alternatives, or trying to solve a specific problem. Not broad informational queries. Technical audit: We find and fix what's holding your existing content back. Crawl issues, page speed, structured data, internal linking. Content strategy: Topic clusters built around buying-intent stages. Every piece has a purpose in the pipeline. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization): We structure content so it gets cited by AI tools. ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews are now part of the B2B buyer research process. Attribution: Monthly report tracking rankings, organic traffic, and leads attributed to organic search. You see which content is driving demos. What organic SEO produces for B2B SaaS. 702% Average SEO ROI for B2B SaaS companies First Page Sage, 2025 7 months Average break-even point for B2B SaaS SEO investment $290 Long-term organic CAC as content compounds vs $802 for paid search FROM THE WORK Archive LinkedIn Content Strategy 7,363 impressions · 1.21% engagement rate maintained Read case study → Archive Radar Feature Launch 2.45% engagement rate · 7 brands to waitlist Read case study → How it works. STEP 1 Pilot (30 days) Technical audit. Keyword gap analysis. We identify the 3 to 5 highest-priority opportunities and begin execution. STEP 2 Strategy Full keyword architecture, topic clusters, and content calendar. You approve before we write anything. STEP 3 Publish and measure Monthly report covering rankings, organic traffic, and leads attributed. We optimize based on what's moving. Organic SEO pairs with content marketing in the Engine and Full Stack tiers. Start with the pilot to see what the opportunity looks like for your specific domain. Pilot: $1,500 to $2,000 for 30 days. Full Stack tier (includes technical SEO + GEO): $6,000/month. Start my 30-day pilot → See full pricing → Questions you probably have. How long until we see results? Do you write the content too?
